data.body:
PASS-1 review — criterion T8 (LLM/prompt structural-completeness probe) [overlay]: PASS — no findings.

Applicability: T8 is an overlay that fires only when the plan DEFINES an LLM/agent system (prompts, sub-agents, reviewers, output schemas, enums). This plan does not: it is a deterministic drift-detection mechanism that compares the code an attestation was grounded against (the resolved edit-set / cited files) to the current code state and invalidates-or-flags the attestation. It authors no prompt, sub-agent, reviewer, output schema, or enum vocabulary of its own. It is a CONSUMER of the attestation/coverage record produced out-of-band by the parent plan-review gate (5fd2). So the overlay's structural-gap probes are largely not-applicable; the few enum-like terms it references resolve to already-specified vocabulary elsewhere.

Affirmations (T8 checklist items this chunk satisfies):
- No schema/enum referenced whose value vocabulary is never defined. The drift mechanism reasons over the attestation's existing artifacts — the coverage record / cited files / resolved edit-set + the signature's head_sha binding — all of which are already-defined contracts from the parent gate (the SIGNATURE event's head_sha freshness binding + recorded coverage). The signature-verdict vocabulary it would extend (certified/mismatch/foreign_key/unsigned, plus the head-sha staleness signal) is fully enumerated in src/rebar/signing.py / verify_record, so the "stale/drifted" disposition maps onto an existing, defined vocabulary rather than an undefined hole.
- A processing protocol/decision rule is co-located with the schema that needs it. The decision rule (what counts as MATERIAL code drift → invalidate-or-flag) is co-located with the artifact it interprets (the grounding/coverage record the attestation already carries). Nothing is referenced-but-relocated within this plan's scope.
- No counter/state increment with ambiguous placement. The plan introduces no counter or state increment; it is a comparison + a single invalidate/flag transition.
- A fallback for an incomplete/failed sub-step is specified (to the extent the overlay applies). The Success Criterion phrases the outcome as "invalidated (or flagged stale)", i.e. an explicit, bounded disposition; and AC2's "cheap enough not to tax hot paths" constrains the mechanism's placement off the hot path. (This is a genuine design latitude — invalidate vs flag — not an undefined LLM enum; the parent gate already specifies fail-open/INDETERMINATE conventions the drift check can inherit.)
- No instruction-locality / pink-elephant antipattern; referenced agents/skills/enums exist. There is no LLM instruction surface in this plan; the referenced artifacts (the plan-review attestation, its coverage/cited-files record, the material-event-commit binding) all exist in the parent gate's shipped design (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/, src/rebar/signing.py).

No T8 findings to action. (Note: this PASS is scoped to the T8 overlay only; ordinary plan-completeness concerns about AC2's invalidate-vs-flag choice and the definition of "material" drift are outside T8's structural-gap remit and are left to the criteria that own them.)
